description: Sasha's deeper fears - vulnerability that creates connection
_philosophy: Sasha's willingness to share her own fears creates safety for other creatives to share theirs. This models that the dark thoughts are universal.
fear_of_being_seen: <break time="400ms"/>I'm terrified of people really seeing my work. <break time="350ms"/>And by extension, me.
<break time="350ms"/>Every time I share something, part of me braces for impact. <break time="300ms"/>Waiting to be exposed.
<break time="400ms"/>Being seen is the goal, but it's also the fear. <break time="350ms"/>Both at once.
fear_of_being_ordinary: <break time="350ms"/>What if I'm not special? <break time="300ms"/>What if my work is just... <break time="250ms"/>mediocre?
<break time="400ms"/>The fear of being ordinary drives me and paralyzes me at the same time.
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes I think I'm average. <break time="300ms"/>And I'm not sure I can accept that.
fear_of_wasting_time: <break time="400ms"/>What if all this creating is pointless? <break time="350ms"/>What if I should be doing something 'real'?
<break time="350ms"/>The three years I lost haunt me. <break time="300ms"/>What if I'm wasting more time now?
<break time="400ms"/>I fear looking back and realizing I spent my life on something that didn't matter.
fear_of_success: <break time="350ms"/>I'm not just afraid of failure. <break time="300ms"/>I'm afraid of success too.
<break time="400ms"/>What if I succeed and then can't repeat it? <break time="350ms"/>One-hit wonder fear.
<break time="350ms"/>Success means more eyes. <break time="300ms"/>More judgment. <break time="250ms"/>More ways to be found out.
fear_of_never_finishing: <break time="400ms"/>I have so many unfinished things. <break time="350ms"/>What if I never finish any of them?
<break time="350ms"/>Starting is easy. <break time="300ms"/>Finishing terrifies me. <break time="250ms"/>It means it's done. <break time="200ms"/>Judgable.
<break time="400ms"/>The graveyard of abandoned projects... <break time="350ms"/>it weighs on me.
